expressions.english: friend(ly) relation; friends
a couple
close relation
expressions.notes: 친하다 = to be close (with other people)

expressions.english:
to have a good relationship
to get along well
expressions.notes: 좋다 = to be good, likable; to be desirable; to be nice; to like

expressions.english:
to have a bad relationship
to not get along well
How do you know each other?
You two know each other?
expressions.notes:
둘 = two (native Korean number)
두 is the adjective form of 둘.

expressions.english: We are just friends.
expressions.notes:
저희 = (polite) we; us; our
그냥 = just, only, simply

expressions.english: What kind of relationship do you have with him/her?

expressions.korean: 우리 사이에 못 할 말이 어디 있어요?
expressions.english:
What words are there that cannot be spoken between us?
Is there anything we can't tell each other?
expressions.notes: 할(을) 하다 = to say, speak, tell, talk

expressions.english: I have a good relationship with my parents.
Who do you get along with the best out of the people here?
expressions.notes:
여기 = here, this place
누구 = who
제일 = the best; the most

expressions.korean: 친구 사이에 부끄러움 게 뭐가 있어요?
expressions.english: What is there to be embarrassed about between friends?
expressions.notes: 부끄럽다 = to be ashamed; to be shameful; to be embarrassed; to be embarrassing

expressions.korean: 그 사람하고 아는 사이인 줄 몰랐어요.
expressions.english: I didn't know you were acquainted with her.
expressions.notes:
-은 줄 모르다 = to not know/notice + S + be
+ N

expressions.korean: 룸메이트하고 사이 좋게 잘 지내고 있어요.
expressions.english:
I am getting along well with my roommate.
I have a good report with my roommate.
I have a good relationship with my roommate.
expressions.notes:
룸메이트 = roommate
사이 좋게 지내다 = to get along

expressions.korean: 그 사람은 친구들 사이에서 인기가 많아요.
expressions.english: He is popular among his friends.

expressions.korean: 자는 제 여동생하고 사이가 별로 안 좋아요.
expressions.english:
I don't have a very good relationship with my younger sister.
I don't get along well with my younger sister.
expressions.notes:
여동생 = younger sister
별로 = not particularly, not very, not so much, not really

expressions.korean: 그 두 사람은 사이가 별로 안 좋은 것 같아요.
expressions.english: It seemed like the two people did not have a very good relationship.

expressions.korean: 그 두 사람이 그렇게 사이가 좋은 줄 몰랐어요.
expressions.english: I didn't know those two were in such a good relationship.
expressions.notes:
그렇게 = like that; such + adjective/adverb
-는 줄 모르다 = to not know/notice + S + V

expressions.korean: 지난번에 그 사람하고 싸워서 사이가 안 좋아졌어요.
expressions.english:
I argued with him last time, and the relationship became bad.
I argued with him last time, and the relationship took a turn for the worse.
